prerequisites: Network access to the SpaceX-API service at the base URL https://api.spacexdata.com. No account, API key, or credentials are required for read-only access.
Awareness that this is an unofficial, community-maintained API (the r-spacex/SpaceX-API project) and not affiliated with SpaceX. As of June 2026 the source repository is archived and read-only.
Optionally, knowledge of the document id you want to fetch (the 'id' config field) when retrieving a single record instead of a full collection.

Optionally, a query 'options' object (supporting fields like select, sort, limit, page, offset, and populate) when you want to control pagination or shape the returned data (the 'options' config field).

authMethods.name: No authentication (public read access)
authMethods.summary: Read-only access to the SpaceX-API requires no authentication. The connector defines no secret fields. Per the official docs, an API key (passed as a 'spacex-key' header) is only required for destructive create, update, and delete routes, which a read-only source does not use. GET and POST /query read requests work without any key.

setupSteps.title: Confirm connectivity to the SpaceX API
setupSteps.body: Point the connector at the public base URL https://api.spacexdata.com. No credentials are needed: read endpoints (GET collection routes and POST /query routes) are open. You can verify access with an unauthenticated request to any read route, for example the launches or rockets collection.

setupSteps.title: Set the record id (optional)
setupSteps.body: Leave the 'id' config field empty to extract a full collection. To fetch a single document, set 'id' to that record's identifier, which the connector uses against the single-document route (GET /:id).

setupSteps.title: Configure query options (optional)
setupSteps.body: Use the 'options' config field to pass query options to the API's POST /query route. The API supports 'select' (fields to return), 'sort', pagination via 'limit'/'page'/'offset', and 'populate' (to load referenced documents). The query response includes pagination metadata such as totalDocs, page, and hasNextPage.
